name: Creamy, Fudgy, Nutty Brownies
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Nuts (e.g., walnuts or pecans), chopped and toasted
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, melt the unsweetened chocolate and margarine or butter over low heat, stirring occasionally. Remove from heat; cool.
2. Grease and lightly flour an 8x8x2-inch baking pan; set aside.
3. In a medium mixing bowl, stir together the flour, chopped nuts, and baking powder; set aside.
4. In a large mixing bowl, stir together the cooled melted chocolate mixture and sugar. Add the eggs and vanilla extract. Using a wooden spoon, lightly beat the mixture just until combined (do not overbeat).
5. Stir in the flour mixture. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and spread to edges.
6. Bake at 350°F for 40 minutes.
7. Meanwhile, for topping: In a small saucepan, melt semisweet or bittersweet chocolate over low heat, stirring occasionally; cool slightly.
8. In a medium mixing bowl, beat cream cheese with an electric mixer on medium speed about 30 seconds until softened. Add the melted chocolate, egg, sugar, milk, and vanilla extract. Beat until combined.
9. Carefully spread topping evenly over hot brownies.
10. Bake at 350°F for another 10 minutes or until the topping is set. Cool in the pan on a wire rack.
11. Cover; chill at least 2 hours before serving.
12. To serve, cut the brownies into triangles or bars (cut rectangles first, then diagonally). If desired, melt 2 squares of chocolate and shortening in a small saucepan and drizzle over the brownies and plate. Cover and refrigerate to store. Makes 12 to 16 servings.
